name: Cross-Encoder (Bi-encoder Reranking)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); UKPLab / Nils Reimers; part of sentence-transformers

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace token for private models

performance.notes: Slower than bi-encoder (no precomputation); GPU needed for reranking large candidate sets

agent_readiness.notes: Cross-encoder models in sentence-transformers package for reranking. CrossEncoder class takes (query, passage) pairs and outputs relevance scores. Significantly more accurate than bi-encoders for relevance ranking. Standard RAG reranking pipeline: bi-encoder retrieves top-K candidates, cross-encoder reranks for accuracy. Models: ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2, cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base. SentenceTransformer also provides bi-encoder + cross-encoder combo workflows.

gotchas: Cannot precompute document embeddings (full pair re-encoding at query time)
Quadratic cost — only rerank small candidate sets (50-200 docs)
Different models optimized for different domains
Must combine with bi-encoder retrieval for scalable RAG
best_when: RAG pipelines needing high-accuracy reranking after bi-encoder retrieval
Search quality improvement over pure embedding similarity
Document relevance scoring for specific queries
avoid_when: Ranking thousands of documents directly (too slow)
Use cases where bi-encoder accuracy is sufficient
summary: Cross-Encoders from sentence-transformers provide high-accuracy relevance reranking for RAG pipelines. CrossEncoder takes (query, passage) pairs and scores relevance. More accurate than bi-encoders but cannot precompute — only practical for reranking small candidate sets (50-200 docs). Standard two-stage retrieval: bi-encoder retrieves → cross-encoder reranks. Dramatically improves RAG answer quality vs cosine similarity alone.
